Petition To Determine Validity Of Amendments To Trust And To Impose Constructive Trust
Off Calendar Dec-28-2015 Text Ruling

Petition To Determine Validity Of Amendments To Trust And To Impose Constructive Trust
Off Calendar Oct-07-2015 Continued To Nov-20-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Emilie Calhoun for and with Paula Gerhardt and for Gloria Gregg. Richard Collier for (petitioner) Suandra Meyrose.

The Court stated that an evidentiary hearing is needed and asked counsel for their time frame. Counsel agreed that 3 hours would be sufficient. The petition to determine validity of amendments to trust and to impose constructive trust was continued to November 20, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court instructed counsel to file their witness and exhibit lists as well as trial briefs no later than November 13, 2015.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Watts. (Not Reported)

Petition To Determine Validity Of Amendments To Trust And To Impose Constructive Trust
Off Calendar Sep-14-2015 Continued To Sep-28-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records By Kaiser Permanente
Off Calendar Oct-07-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Emilie Calhoun for and with (petitioner) Paula Gerhardt. Richard Collier for the respondents.

Ms. Calhoun stated that they are contesting the production of medical records, that there is a hearing on Monday on the underlying petition, that they would like to be sent out to trial, that they would like to put a halt on discovery that is not relative, that the records requested contain private financial information, that delving into privacy at this juncture is wasteful, that Mr. Collier is asserting that the privacy privilege is personal and expires at death and cited 993(c). The Court asked Mr. Collier what it would help to focus on that part of the case at this point. Mr. Collier responded that the patient/client privilege expired quite some time ago, that their petition has been on file for over a year and that they are entitled to discovery. The Court asked if he could get a global resolution without it. Mr. Collier that in the absence of discovery they only have the observations of the client. The Court stated that there is no problem with entitlement and asked if there was any prudential reasons to wait until after the first phase. Mr. Collier stated that he offered to limit review discovery to non parties, that his clients were suspicious of the excerpts that were provided and that they do not want to go forward if there is not a chance of winning. The Court stated that it could stick with the tentative and maybe put a freeze on things, that if it is favorable it may prevent them from going forward and presented several scenarios. Ms. Calhoun stated that they have a large stack of documents, some of it contains information that is personal and embarrassing that they would not want public, that they do not want the documents shared with Mr. Collier's clients, that Lynne Searle was at the signing and could speak to what she observed, that the core issue is capacity at the time of signing and if there was an assessment done, that all of this has been provided to Mr. Collier, that her clients have a duty to protect Mr. Moore and cited a case regarding the privacy privilege. Mr. Collier stated that the underlying petition is set for Monday and that they are not ready to go forward based on this hearing. The Court asked if there was a way to reveal pertinent documents, excluding those that are in no way relevant, that the spirit would be to disclose as many documents as possible, a deadline of 9/24/15 would be given and if the court has not heard from the parties it will rule; the matter would be submitted as of 9/24/15. Ms. Calhoun stated that it was a brilliant idea, that her and Mr. Collier could work out a protective order and requested a 5-minute recess to confer with her client. Ms. Calhoun reported back that her client is in accord with the suggestion, that she will work with Mr. Collier on a protective order and that she has the Kaiser documents at her office. The Motion to quash deposition subpoena for production of business records by Kaiser Permanente is taken under submission as of September 24, 2015.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Clarke. (Not Reported)

DEMURRER
Off Calendar Mar-24-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Emilie Calhoun & David Parnall for (trustees/respondents) Gloria Gregg & Paula Gerhardt. Richard Collier for (petitioners) Saundra Meyrose, Robin Williams, Lesley Paul & Grace Strosnider.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to what date notice was received and what date documents were received, what date actually triggers the 120 days, who relief was being sought against and if this is a trust contest. The court took the Demurrer under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

Off Calendar Nov-06-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Emilie Calhoun for (demurring parties) Paula Gerhardt & Gloria Gregg. Richard Collier for (respondents) Suandra Meyrose, Robin Williams, Lesley Paul & Grace Strosnider.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their case. A proposed order was submitted, the matter was taken under submission with an order to be issued no later than November 10, 2014.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: None. (Not Reported)
